question: What is the title of the research study on page 1 that investigates the issue of inadequate drainage systems and flooding risk in Barangay Triangulo, Naga City?
contexts:
["--- Page 1 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: April 2025
Title: LoRaWAN-Integrated Non-Contact Drainage Monitoring System in Barangay Triangulo,
Naga City
Authors: *
● Lillo, Charles Benedict I.
● Ocampo, Ivan Khristopher C.
● San Buenaventura, Zhevastian Luise P.
Abstract:
This study investigates the pressing issue of inadequate drainage systems and the resulting
flooding risk to human lives and public health, particularly in flood-prone areas. By monitoring
the drainage system of Brgy. Triangulo, Naga City, Philippines, it introduces a non-contact
drainage monitoring system that detects blockages, monitors water levels and surface velocity,
and provides real-time data via the ThingsPH Internet of Things (IoT) platform. Moreover, the
system integrates Long Range Wide Area Network (LoRaWAN) technology with radar
technology for non-contact measurement, which is an effective solution for drainage monitoring.
This non-contact drainage monitoring system integrated with LoRaWAN technology includes an
embedded microcontroller with integrated sensors. It follows the programming of device
operations using C++, while the LoRaWAN gateway, network server, application server, and IoT
platform were configured on their respective domains.
Keywords: drainage systems, LoRaWAN technology, non-contact measurement, surface
velocity, water level

--- Page 2 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: January 2025
Title: DEVELOPMENT OF A DEHYDRATOR MACHINE FOR GABI LEAVES USING
ELECTRIC HEATER SYSTEM AND SUPPORTING SOLAR HEAT CATCHER
Authors:
● Jech Ivan D. Audal
● Vince Robert N. De Las Llagas
Abstract:
Sun drying has been used to prepare and preserve various agricultural products, extending the
shelf life of said products such as Gabi leaves in the Philippines. Many farmers dry their yield
through the traditional method. However, sun drying tends to be labor-intensive and has poor
quality control. Leaves are also prone to contaminants since they are dried in the open air.
Temperature control is also difficult in this method and leaves can easily be overheated causing
low quality. It is also not possible to dry at night or during rain. This is efficient only when
conditions, like dry weather and available space, are met. The use of a dehydrator provides an
alternate means that would not need to meet such conditions, and thus the machine makes the
drying process readily available. The researchers designed a dehydrator that is an efficient
alternative to sun drying of gabi leaves which makes use of a solar heat catcher to provide heat
in a controlled environment to yield an ideal dehydrated state of gabi leaves. An embedded
microcontroller will be used to integrate a control system that uses temperature as a feedback
mechanism. The system will use fans and an electric heating system for error value corrections.
The embedded microcontroller will also have WiFi connectivity for the IoT interface.

--- Page 3 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering and Computer Engineering
Date: May 2025
Title: Design and Monitoring of an IoT-based Hydroponic System with Automated pH Dosing
using Fuzzy PID Control
Authors: Lyan Shamira T. Dacoycoy, Tiffany Moso, John Miguel C. Moll
Abstract:
This study explored the implementation and evaluation of an IoT-based hydroponic system
using an A-frame Nutrient Film Technique (NFT) to grow lettuce, comparing manual and
automated pH dosing. Hydroponics enables efficient, sustainable plant growth by allowing
precise control over nutrients, pH, and water. Manual pH adjustment, though common, is prone
to human error and inconsistencies, potentially harming plant health. In contrast, the automated
system employed a Fuzzy PID controller, integrating fuzzy logic with traditional PID control to
maintain stable pH levels by adjusting dosing pumps based on sensor feedback. Environmental
data including pH, EC, temperature, and humidity—was monitored using sensors connected to
an ESP32 microcontroller, with a mobile and web app (built with Flutter and Google Sheets API)
providing up-to-date monitoring. Results from two trials showed that automated dosing
consistently kept pH within the optimal 5.5-6.5 range, outperforming manual control. Statistical
analysis using the Mann-Whitney U test indicated significantly better plant growth in the
automated system across most parameters, including leaf count, size, and fresh weight, while
ANOVA showed no significant difference in dry weight. These findings demonstrated that
automated pH regulation enhances plant growth, reduces labor, and improves cost-efficiency in
hydroponic farming.
Keywords: hydroponics, Internet of Things (IoT), pH dosing, Fuzzy PID

--- Page 4 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: April 2025
Title: IoT-Based Surface Methane Emissions Monitoring System with Environmental Data
Visualization in San Isidro Naga City Sanitary Landfill
Authors: Karl Shandrick A. Bathan, Paul C. Enriquez
Abstract:
Methane emissions from landfills pose serious environmental and health risks, contributing to
global warming and increasing the likelihood of fires and explosions. This study developed and
tested an IoT-based surface methane emissions monitoring system at the San Isidro Naga City
Sanitary Landfill, where no such system previously existed. The system integrated
environmental sensors to monitor methane concentration, wind speed and direction,
temperature, and humidity. Data were transmitted via a LoRa network and mobile data to an IoT
platform for visualization and storage. The system operated continuously throughout the test
period, demonstrating its capability for sustained monitoring and enhancing community safety.
Keywords:
Methane Monitoring, San Isidro Naga City Sanitary Landfill, IoT platform, LoRa network

--- Page 5 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: June 2023
Title: Emergency Alert System for Notifying the Fire Station and CDRRMO in Naga City through
Radio Frequency, SMS, Camera, and IoT Integration
Authors: Claude Allan M. Garcera, Timothie Xena S. Vasquez, Faith Angela T. Zuniega
Abstract:
The researchers have developed an alert system to address challenges faced during
emergencies in Naga City. The system is designed to overcome the limitations of traditional
communication methods such as phones which may not always be reliable during emergencies.
The proposed device functions by utilizing radio frequency to automatically notify and call the
Fire Station and Naga City Disaster Risk Reduction And Management Office. The device is
equipped with a camera for security purposes and includes a text messaging feature which
transmits messages to the authorities containing the location of the device and details about the
type of emergency requiring assistance. The researchers conducted 30 simulation tests, 15
tests for each of the two buttons for fire and rescue. The statistical treatments of data used are
the Mean, Standard Deviation, and Independent Sample t-test to analyze the gathered data and
arrive in a logical conclusion of the study. The average time recorded for sending a text
message using the device is 10.35 seconds, while sending a message through cell phone is
32.79 seconds. Overall, the researchers were able to develop a device that demonstrates the
effectiveness and feasibility of a designed alert system emphasizing the potential for improving
coordination between authorities and residents during emergencies.
Keywords: Emergency Alert System, Radio Frequency, ThingSpeak, Internet of Things,
Camera Integration.

--- Page 6 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: June 2023
Title: Wireless Sensor Network for Real-Time Monitoring and Alerting of Air Conditioning Units
Authors: Bon Joey I. Pintang, Melvin D. Sta. Rosa, Jr., Johannes Andrei A. Unana
Abstract:
This research designed a remote monitoring and alert system for air conditioning units. Two
identical prototypes were designed for two separate rooms. Each prototype used an ESP32
development board installed with sensors to measure temperature, humidity, vibration, current,
and freon levels. A Raspberry Pi is installed with a LAMP stack and was configured in the same
local network as the prototype. A prototype uploads its gathered monitoring data to the local
database server. In the event of an unusual reading, the system sends an email alert
notification, including relevant sensor readings and their statuses, to the email addresses stored
in the database. An Android mobile application was also developed for real-time monitoring of
sensor data. In the statistical testing conducted, both prototypes monitored and uploaded
70,538 rows of data over a five-day period. The collected data were subjected to statistical
analysis using a two-sample t-test. This resulted in a significant difference in the humidity and
temperature tests but none in the current and vibration tests. The differences in humidity and
temperature tests may be attributed to various factors like other environmental conditions.
Keywords: Remote monitoring, Internet of things, Wireless sensor network, Air conditioning,
Sensors, Mobile application, Alert notification.

--- Page 7 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: June 2023
Title: Wireless Sensor Network for Real-Time Monitoring and Alerting of Air Conditioning Units
Authors: Bon Joey I. Pintang, Melvin D. Sta. Rosa, Jr., Johannes Andrei A. Unana
Abstract:
This research designed a remote monitoring and alert system for air conditioning units. Two
identical prototypes were designed for two separate rooms. Each prototype used an ESP32
development board installed with sensors to measure temperature, humidity, vibration, current,

Keywords: Remote monitoring, Internet of things, Wireless sensor network, Air conditioning,

--- Page 8 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: February 2024
Title: Wireless Sensor Node Data Gathering and Location Mapping for Environmental
Monitoring Using LoRaWAN in Naga City
Authors: Andrei Chrys A. Aspe, Royce C. Gnilo, Jesus Ricardo M. Sierra
Abstract:
Wireless technology has evolved since the discovery of electromagnetic waves, finding
applications across various industries and military sectors. Its capabilities enable IoT
applications, environmental monitoring, and real-time sensor and microcontroller
communication. This study proposes a LoRaWAN sensor network for environmental monitoring
in Naga City, Camarines Sur, focusing on key environmental parameters such as PM2.5, PM10,
CO2, noise, temperature, and humidity while ensuring compliance with city environmental
ordinances. The Naga City Office of Environment and Natural Resources (ENRO) oversees
these efforts city-wide. Researchers have designed a system using LoRaWAN communication
between nodes and gateways, enabling remote data collection. Gateways serve as an access
point for the solar-powered sensor nodes. Gateways utilize WiFi to send information to the
MQTT server. The sensor units comprise microcontrollers, sensors, GPS, and LoRa modules
programmed to gather and transmit data to the Gateway. Field tests were conducted to validate
the performance and functionality of the data transmission. IoT communication was evaluated
by placing gateways in various city locations. The collected data is processed and visualized
through QGIS using a Python plugin, offering valuable insights into environmental quality and
regulatory compliance.
Keywords: LoRaWAN, Environmental monitoring, Naga City, IoT applications, Sensor network

--- Page 9 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: May 2023
Title: Commuter Bicycle Fall Detection System with Emergency Alert System
Authors: Francis Aldrin Y. Belmes, Paul Adrian P. Delos Santos, Klient Jeremy B. Sambo
Abstract:
This paper presents a study on bicycle fall detection and emergency alert system by designing a
prototype that measures the signal vector magnitude for acceleration and signal vector
magnitude for tilt. The increasing popularity of bicycles as a means of transportation has led to a
rise in bicycle-related accidents, posing a critical problem for cyclists worldwide. The integration
of such systems into bicycles can play a vital role in mitigating the impact of accidents and
ensuring timely assistance. In this paper, a commuter bicycle underwent 348 trials simulating
falls in four directions (left, right, forward, and backward) and four placements (A: seat post, B:
top tube frame near the handlebar, C: center of the downtube frame, D: seat tube frame). The
results indicated minimal variation in fall thresholds for acceleration (1.5g to 2.2g) but notable
variability in tilt angles (30° to 70°). Position C exhibited the most suitable placement,
demonstrating low false positive and false negative rates. These findings enhance
understanding of bicycle fall dynamics and inform device installation.
Keywords: fall detection system, emergency alert system, bicycle, threshold, sensors

--- Page 10 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: May 2023
Title: HERO System: An Integration of Machine Learning-based Camera and Threshold-based
Wearable Device for Geriatric Fall Monitoring System
Authors: Neil Angelo C. Bellen, James Lemuel R. Mallapre, Miggi Jafet N. Relato
Abstract:
Falls are the leading cause of injury-related death among elderly people, hence devices that
could detect it before it happens will be very beneficial for the elderly people. This study is
focused on designing and developing an integrated fall monitoring system using AND operation
- a machine learning-based (ML-based) camera known as Highly Efficient Real-time Operating
(HERO) Cam, and a threshold-based wearable device called Highly Efficient Real-time
Operating (HERO) Band. The integration of two devices forms the Highly Efficient Real-time
Operating (HERO) System. This system has the developed performance and overall efficiency
that enables effective monitoring, detection, and real-time responses. A Wi-Fi push notification
shall be received by the family members or assigned medical attendants when a fall is detected.
With the data obtained by the researchers from various experiments conducted, the HERO Cam
achieved an accuracy of 93.50%, the HERO Band demonstrated an accuracy of 92.67%, and
the HERO System obtained the highest accuracy among the two systems with 97.36%. It can
be concluded that the HERO System proved to be successful and accurate in detecting falls.
Keywords: Fall Monitoring System, Threshold-based, Machine Learning, Wi-Fi Push
Notification, Geriatric Fall

--- Page 11 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: QECC220 (Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering context)
Date: August 2023
Title: Illegal Parking Detection Using Machine Learning with Wireless Alarm System and SMS
Notification
Authors: Justine Raya A. Adoremos, Vanduane F. Bailey, Joshua M. Velasco
Abstract:
Illegal Parking has been an issue for decades on every highway and more particularly, on small
alley roads being the cause of traffic and the traffic itself is one of the arising problems or
concerns in Naga City. This study made a system that will help minimize or prevent the busy
roads of Naga City from having illegally parked vehicles. Closed-Circuit Television (CCTV)
footage was subjected to image processing techniques such as gray scaling, cropping, and
resizing which serves as the datasets for object detection model using a machine learning
algorithm called You Only Look Once version 5 (YOLOv5). This machine learning algorithm was
then integrated into vehicle detection and wireless communication technology for the alarm and
notification system using LoRaWAN and GSM. The machine learning algorithm used by the
researchers to identify illegally parked vehicles gave a mean average precision of 99.23% with
IoU or Intersection over Union thresholding value of 0.5 and 83.4% with IoU or Intersection over
Union thresholding ranging value from 0.5 to 0.95. Furthermore, wireless communication
technologies were developed for the interconnectivity of LoRa based Alarm System (LBAS),
LoRa Vehicle Information System (LVIS), and LoRa GSM Base Station (LGBS). Upon final
testing, the study helped the Public Safety Officer to maximize the area of responsibility through
the system made by the researchers.
Keywords: Illegal Parking, LGBS, LBAS, LVIS, detecting, YOLO, wireless, notification

--- Page 12 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: September 2025
Title: Experimental Evaluation of Multi-Floor Localization Using Ultra-Wideband (UWB)
Technology with ESP32 Microcontrollers
Authors: Sherwin A. Beraquit Jr., Ma. Dolores P. Beron, Dante M. Joson
Abstract:
High infrastructure cost and complex signal propagation are the most common challenges that
significantly affect a multi-floor localization system with high accuracy and scalability. Designing
and evaluating a multi-floor indoor localization system that integrates low-cost Ultra-Wideband
(UWB) technology with ESP32 microcontrollers is implemented by this study to address the
challenges. The system was deployed, and experiments were conducted on the second and
third floors of the Dolan Building at Ateneo de Naga University. Hallway and floor-level
performance were assessed using four statistical metrics: Average Circular Error (ACE) for
accuracy, Interquartile Range (IQR) for precision, Coefficient of Variation (CV) for stability, and
95% Confidence Interval (CI) for reliability. Exceptional sub-meter ACEs of 0.615 m and 0.616 m
were achieved by the side hallways of the second and third floors, respectively, confirming high
accuracy with lower ACEs. Higher errors attributed to increased multipath interference are
exhibited by the center hallways. Acceptable overall accuracy (ACE = 0.870 m for the second
floor and ACE = 0.810 m for the third floor) is shown for both floors, with good stability (CV
values of 0.640-0.650) and reliability (CI values of ±0.040 m). This study affirms the feasibility of
ESP32-based UWB systems for effective multi-floor localization while highlighting the need to
consider specific environmental conditions.
Keywords: multi-floor localization, ESP32, ultra-wideband, localization accuracy, trilateration

--- Page 13 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: November 2024
Title: IoT-based Water Monitoring System: Integration of ATmega644p and ATmega328p for
Irrigation Water Level and Water Quality Monitoring, using SIM900A Lora-Gateway to
ThingSpeakIoT
Authors: Ramon S. Ocampo II, Ken Matthew L. de Leon, John Richard S. Tuico
Abstract:
Irrigation Systems are crucial in mitigating water scarcity and improving agricultural productivity.
Effective management necessitates the continuous monitoring of water levels and quality. This
study introduces an IoT-based monitoring system to measure key irrigation parameters. Using
ATmega644p and ATmega328p microcontrollers with various sensors data is transmitted via
SIM900A LoRa-Gateway to a ThingSpeak Interface for real-time analysis. The interface stores
data and provides visualizations and insights for efficient irrigation management. This system
represents a significant advancement in irrigation management as it ensures accurate
monitoring, timely data, and informed recommendations, thereby helping to optimize irrigation
practices and contribute to sustainable agricultural productivity and resource management. The
project also features a pump irrigation system to transfer water to an elevated stilling pool,
which helps in preventing waterlogging and drought through controlled water distribution.
Keywords: Irrigation System, Monitoring System, Water Quality, Water Level, IoT,
ATmega644p, ATmega328p, SIM900A, LoRa-Gateway, ThingSpeakIoT, Real-time Data

--- Page 14 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering and Computer Engineering
Date: June 2023
Title: Improved Web-based Surveillance and Monitoring System for NOCS Data Center with
Optimized Performance and Storage
Authors: Vince Mitchell A. Lina, Ojmar Caezar A. Manalang, Ma. Vivien M. Angeles
Abstract:
The previous monitoring system employed in the university's Network Operation & Computer
Services (NOCS) has shown effectiveness in overseeing the Data Center. However, there is still
room for improvement to the system. In this study, the researchers proposed enhancements to
the previous project such as developing a user-friendly and configurable web-based monitoring
and surveillance design, using SHT31 temperature and humidity sensors for more accurate
reading, etc. Considering the limited documentation for the water and smoke sensors, these
modules will be tested. A Z-test with a critical value of 5% was used for the accuracy
assessment. The assessment revealed that the water sensor met the 90% accuracy standard,
while the smoke sensor failed due to the smoke being hovered a few inches away from the
sensor, resulting in only 74% accuracy. To address the identified limitations in the previous
system, solutions are proposed such as, uninterruptible power supply (UPS) for unpredictable
power outages, enable network connections beyond local access allowing remote monitoring to
access the system, etc. Further improvements can be explored to enhance the overall
effectiveness of the monitoring system.
Keywords: Data Center, Monitoring System, Surveillance, Network, Web-Based, IoT

--- Page 15 ---
Institution: Ateneo de Naga University
Program: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering
Date: June 2023
Title: IoT-Enabled Automatic Water Meter System with Valve Actuation for Water Meter
Management in Naga City
Authors: Carmela N. Imperial, Kim Kenneth L. Kinkito, Toam Benjamin G. Secundo
Abstract:
Water meter management has historically been neglected, despite its significant impact on utility
income due to errors in consumption measurement. Naga City faces challenges such as
misplaced bills, leakage, and meter miscalibration. A Webserver Internet of Things (IoT) based
project was developed to address these issues. The device utilizes a water flow sensor,
solenoid valve, Node Microcontroller Unit, Long Range (LoRa) Module, and a limit switch. Data
exchange occurs through a star topology with one hub and three slaves. The device follows ISO
4064 standards for accuracy and employs a volumetric technique for flow measurement in
constrained conduits. Calibration involved five points with twenty readings each, maintaining a
constant flow rate. The device achieved an accuracy of 98.63%, making it a potential
replacement for existing water meters.
Keywords: Automatic water meter, Internet of Things, Actuation, ESP32, LoRa, Water Flow,
Solenoid Valve"]
